topics.name: 100 Level: Introduction to Accounting & Business (EBSU)
topics.description: ACC 101 Financial Accounting I, BUS 111 Business Organisation, ECO 101 Economics for Management.

subtopics.name: ACC 101: Accounting Fundamentals at EBSU
objectives: Record financial transactions in the journal and post to the general ledger using double-entry principles
Prepare trial balance and basic financial statements for sole traders and small businesses in Ebonyi State context

subtopics.name: ECO 101: Basic Economics for Management Sciences
objectives: Explain demand, supply, and price elasticity concepts relevant to business decision-making
Identify macroeconomic challenges such as inflation and unemployment facing South-East Nigerian businesses

topics.name: 200–300 Level: Intermediate Financial Accounting, Cost & Taxation
topics.description: ACC 201 Intermediate Financial Accounting, ACC 211 Cost Accounting, ACC 301 Taxation.

subtopics.name: ACC 201 & ACC 211: Financial and Cost Accounting at EBSU
objectives: Prepare partnership accounts, company accounts, and cash flow statements per Nigerian GAAP and IFRS
Compute product costs using job costing, process costing, and activity-based costing systems

subtopics.name: ACC 301: Nigerian Tax System
objectives: Calculate personal income tax under the Personal Income Tax Act for Ebonyi State residents
Compute companies income tax, education tax, and VAT for businesses operating in South-East Nigeria

topics.name: 400 Level: Auditing, Advanced Financial Reporting & Research Project
topics.description: ACC 401 Auditing, ACC 411 Advanced Financial Reporting, ACC 499 Final Year Research Project.

subtopics.name: ACC 401 & ACC 411: Auditing and Advanced Reporting
objectives: Plan statutory audits and evaluate internal control systems using risk-based audit methodology
Apply IFRS 15, 16, IAS 12, and IAS 36 to complex financial reporting scenarios

subtopics.name: ACC 499: EBSU Accounting Research Dissertation
objectives: Conduct original accounting research on a topic relevant to South-East Nigerian business and finance
Present and defend dissertation findings before EBSU Faculty of Management Sciences board
